title: Building Resilience, Agency, and Hope: Multiple Narratives on Co-Learning and Co-Creating with Community
presenter: Rula Awwad-Rafferty; Emily Fisher; Jack Wilkens; Krysta Hagen
presenter_affiliation: Interior Architecture & Design, College of Arts & Architecture
description: This workshop examines how institutions can leverage collective expertise to create meaningful, purpose-driven impact through community engagement, co-learning and cocreation. It introduces a practical, hands-on approach grounded in long-term community partnerships and informed by the perspectives of educators, students, and local collaborators.
subject: community engagement;resilience;capstone

title: Abstract: Building Resilience, Agency, and Hope: Multiple Narratives on Co-Learning and Co-Creating with Community
presenter: Rula Awwad-Rafferty; Emily Fisher; Krysta Hagan; Jack Wilkins
presenter_affiliation: Department of Design and Environments, University of Idaho
description: Workshop abstract framing learning, teaching, and success as dynamic, relational activities grounded in the land-grant mission. Presents a hands-on approach to community-engaged co-learning and co-creation drawn from student-led capstone projects and informed by educators, students, and community partners. Highlights peer learning, reciprocal relationships, and shared purpose as foundations for resilience, academic hope, and student agency.
subject: community engagement;co-learning;co-creation;student agency;resilience;academic hope;capstone

title: Slides: Building Resilience, Agency, and Hope: Multiple Narratives on Co-Learning and Co-Creating with Community
presenter: Rula Awwad-Rafferty; Emily Fisher; Krysta Hagan; Jack Wilkins
presenter_affiliation: Department of Design and Environments, University of Idaho
description: Workshop slides presenting community-engaged co-learning and co-creation as a foundation for student, faculty, and community engagement. Features three example projects illustrating peer learning, trust building, and reciprocal relationships with community partners, with practical takeaways including engagement approaches, tools, logistics, and 'magical questions.'
subject: community engagement;co-learning;co-creation;student agency;resilience;academic hope;capstone

title: Bots as Foils: Real Students Learn by Teaching AI Students
presenter_affiliation: Politics and Philosophy, CLASS
description: In this session, the presenter demonstrates an experimental approach to using AI by reversing its typical role. Instead of acting as tutors or assistants, chatbots are intentionally designed to be confused, and students are tasked with helping them understand a topic. This strategy supports customizable assignments for instructors, promotes active learning for students, and builds AI literacy skills.
subject: AI;active learning;AI literacy

title: Abstract: Bots as Foils: Real Students Learn by Teaching AI Students
presenter_affiliation: Politics and Philosophy, CLASS
description: Extended abstract describing the MrBayes platform and Foil Bots approach. Includes survey results from PHIL 1103 (Fall 2025, ~250 students) showing 58% found MrBayes more effective than discussion boards. Covers platform design, persona development, and implications for AI literacy across disciplines.
subject: AI;active learning;AI literacy;chatbot pedagogy

title: Slides: Bots as Foils: Real Students Learn by Teaching AI Students
presenter_affiliation: Politics and Philosophy, CLASS
description: Presentation slides covering the MrBayes platform and Foil Bot approach to AI-assisted learning. Demonstrates how chatbots designed to be confused promote active learning and AI literacy in dialogue-based courses, with survey results from PHIL 1103.
subject: AI;active learning;AI literacy;chatbot pedagogy

title: Transforming Your Class to Utilize Active Learning
presenter_affiliation: Mathematics & Statistical Science, College of Science
description: This workshop is designed for faculty seeking to create more engaging classroom experiences. Participants bring a class day or topic they wish to redesign and collaborate with experienced faculty to explore and apply active learning strategies.
subject: active learning;course design

title: Abstract: Transforming Your Class to Utilize Active Learning Workshop
presenter: Tim Boester; Annelise Nielsen; Molly Sutter
presenter_affiliation: Mathematics and Statistical Sciences, University of Idaho
description: Workshop abstract covering active learning techniques, their benefits, and an example from a mathematics course that recently adopted active learning. Participants discussed how to restructure class days to promote student engagement with course material.
subject: active learning;classroom instruction;student thinking

title: Slides: Transforming Your Class to Utilize Active Learning Workshop
presenter: Tim Boester; Annelise Nielsen; Molly Sutter
presenter_affiliation: Mathematics and Statistical Sciences, University of Idaho
description: Workshop slides presenting active learning techniques, their benefits, and a mathematics course example. Includes discussion prompts for participants to redesign class days using active learning strategies.
subject: active learning;classroom instruction;student thinking

title: Progress Update on Transforming Introductory Math Courses
presenter_affiliation: Mathematics & Statistical Science, College of Science
description: Since Fall 2023, introductory mathematics courses at the University of Idaho have transitioned from the Polya emporium model to a classroom-based approach. This session provides an overview of the changes, examines early outcomes for students and the institution, and discusses ongoing efforts to improve instruction.
subject: math;curriculum reform;outcomes

title: Abstract: Progress Update on Transforming Introductory Mathematics Courses
presenter_affiliation: Mathematics and Statistical Sciences, University of Idaho
description: Extended abstract covering the transformation of Math 1080 and Math 1143 from emporium model to classroom-based active learning since Fall 2023. Discusses investments in personnel, learning spaces, and the Mathematics Assistance Center. Reports decreased DFW rates in Math 1080 and guidance for faculty and advisors on student success indicators.
subject: active learning;mathematics;DFW rates;ALEKS;advising

title: Slides: Progress Update on Transforming Introductory Mathematics Courses
presenter_affiliation: Mathematics and Statistical Sciences, University of Idaho
description: Presentation slides detailing the transformation of Math 1080 and Math 1143 from the Polya emporium model to classroom-based active learning. subject: active learning;mathematics;DFW rates;ALEKS;advising

title: Empowering Students Through Peer Mentoring: A Programmatic Approach to Igniting Connection and Guidance

description: This interactive session highlights peer mentoring as a student-centered approach that enhances teaching, learning, and student success. subject: peer mentoring;pre-health;retention

title: Building a Foundation for Student Success: The First-Year Engineering Experience
presenter_affiliation: Civil & Environmental Engineering, College of Engineering; Chemical & Biological Engineering, College of Engineering
description: This session describes a student-centered engineering course that promotes academic success, professional growth, and identity formation through multidisciplinary, real-world learning experiences. Participants learn how hands-on projects, integrated digital tools, embedded advising, and relationship-building foster belonging and persistence.
subject: engineering;first-year experience;identity

title: Slides: Building a Foundation for Student Success - The First-Year Engineering Experience
presenter_affiliation: Civil & Environmental Engineering, College of Engineering; Chemical & Biological Engineering, College of Engineering
description: Presentation slides covering the evolution of ENGR 1230 First Year Engineering from 2020 to present. Includes course overview, activity schedule, Professional Growth Portfolio structure, group design project details, student feedback data, and instructor reflections. Reports 274 students in 2025-2026 with 89% retention in engineering majors.
subject: engineering;first-year experience;ENGR 1230;design project

title: Mind the Gaps
presenter_affiliation: Academic Support Programs, SEM
description: Have you noticed gaps in the college readiness skills of your students? This presentation discusses the most common college success strategies that Academic Coaches help students with, including time management, active study techniques, critical reading and analysis, and accessing campus resources.
subject: academic coaching;readiness;study skills

title: Inclusive Strategies for Neurodivergent Student Success
presenter_affiliation: Center for Disability, Accessibility & Resources, Dean of Students
description: This interactive workshop provides faculty and staff with practical strategies to support neurodivergent students and promote academic success and belonging. subject: neurodivergence;inclusion;accessibility

title: Faculty Partnership with SI-PASS: Opportunities for Active Learning In and Outside the Classroom
presenter_affiliation: Academic Support Programs, SEM; Biological Sciences, College of Science
description: This interactive workshop explores active learning through Supplemental Instruction-Peer Assisted Study Sessions (SI-PASS), a peer-led program designed to support student success in historically challenging courses. subject: SI-PASS;peer learning;active learning

title: Abstract: Faculty Partnerships with SI-PASS: Opportunities for Active Learning In & Outside the Classroom
presenter_affiliation: Academic Support Programs, University of Idaho; Department of Biological Sciences, University of Idaho
description: Workshop abstract describing strategies used by SI-PASS Leaders (academically skilled peer leaders) to foster active learning in group study sessions, and how faculty can integrate those strategies into large lecture-based courses. Includes reflective activities, a tip sheet, and small-group planning by class size. Notes increases in student academic performance and engagement when active learning strategies are used in courses.
subject: active learning;supplemental instruction;SI-PASS;peer assisted study sessions;student engagement;learning strategies

title: Slides: Faculty Partnerships with SI-PASS: Opportunities for Active Learning In & Outside the Classroom
presenter_affiliation: Academic Support Programs, University of Idaho; Department of Biological Sciences, University of Idaho
description: Workshop slides covering the SI-PASS program model and the active learning techniques used by SI-PASS Leaders in peer-led group study sessions. subject: active learning;supplemental instruction;SI-PASS;peer assisted study sessions;student engagement;learning strategies

title: Gallery Walk Workshop: Collectively Offering Student Success Strategies, Concrete Ideas, and Refinement to our Cross-College Colleagues
presenter: Katie Schiffelbein; Bobbi Hugues; Gabriel Potirniche
presenter_affiliation: Strategic Program and Student, College of Engineering; Chief Strategic Partnerships Officer, College of Engineering; Associate Dean, College of Engineering
description: In this interactive session, College of Engineering staff and faculty share student-centered programs that support belonging, success, persistence, and professional skill development. subject: engineering;cross-college;belonging

title: Virtual Forests and Their Virtual Ecosystems in Mixed Reality: Experiential Learning in VR/AR

subject: VR;AR;experiential learning

title: Why Write? Teaching People in the Age of AI
presenter_affiliation: Writing Center, English Department, CLASS; Writing Center, English Department, CLASS
description: This session examines the impact of generative AI on writing in postsecondary education and reaffirms writing's central role in student learning, achievement, and critical thinking. Participants explore the value of writing for learning within their disciplines and design ready-to-implement writing activities.
subject: writing;AI;critical thinking

title: Abstract: Why Write? Teaching People in the Age of AI
presenter_affiliation: University of Idaho Writing Center
description: Workshop abstract on the value of human-produced writing for student learning, achievement, and metacognition in an era of generative AI. Faculty and student support professionals reflect on writing-to-learn experiences, then design ready-to-implement writing activities through structured discussion, reflection, and small-group brainstorming. Emphasizes ungrading, scaffolding, and student agency in genre and subject matter as best practices.
subject: writing;peer tutoring;efficacy;rhetoric;ethical AI;AI refusal;writing-to-learn

title: Slides: Why Write? Teaching People in the Age of AI
presenter_affiliation: University of Idaho Writing Center
description: Workshop slides presenting the rationale and strategies for incorporating human-produced writing into courses across the disciplines amid the rise of generative AI. Covers writing-to-learn pedagogies, ungrading, scaffolding, and student agency in genre and subject matter, with structured prompts for participant reflection and lesson design.
subject: writing;peer tutoring;efficacy;rhetoric;ethical AI;AI refusal;writing-to-learn

title: 2026 Student Success Symposium Program: Ignite and Elevate
presenter_affiliation: Center for Excellence in Teaching & Learning, University of Idaho
description: Official program for the 2026 University of Idaho Student Success Symposium, featuring the full schedule, session descriptions, and planning committee. Theme: Ignite and Elevate: Teaching for Student Persistence.
subject: student success;persistence;higher education;teaching and learning

title: High-Intensity and Independent Laboratory Research to Enhance Undergraduate Student Engagement and Success
presenter_affiliation: Biological Sciences, College of Science
description: This session outlines the history of undergraduate research productivity in lab-based biological sciences, highlighting student successes and evidence of productivity gains through independent research experiences. It also addresses challenges related to cohort management, future program development, and the integration of AI.
subject: undergraduate research;biology;mentorship

title: Providing the Right Evidence: How Campus Interactions Shape Student Perceptions and Persistence
presenter_affiliation: Advising Services, SEM
description: This interactive session examines how students interpret everyday experiences and feedback as evidence of their abilities and potential. It explores how faculty, staff, and campus partners can intentionally shape classroom interactions, advising conversations, policies, and informal moments to support growth-minded perspectives and student persistence.
subject: advising;growth mindset;persistence

title: Abstract: Providing the Right Evidence: How Campus Interactions Shape Student Perceptions and Persistence
presenter_affiliation: Advising Services, University of Idaho
description: Extended abstract exploring how micro-messages in everyday campus interactions shape students' perceived academic control, motivation, and persistence. Draws on attribution theory, growth mindset, and appreciative advising research. Includes a case study approach and a Micro-Messages that Matter handout.
subject: micro-messaging;perceived academic control;growth mindset;appreciative advising

title: Slides: Providing the Right Evidence: How Campus Interactions Shape Student Perceptions and Persistence
presenter_affiliation: Advising Services, University of Idaho
description: Presentation slides exploring how micro-messages in everyday campus interactions influence students' perceived academic control and persistence. subject: micro-messaging;perceived academic control;growth mindset;appreciative advising

title: The Why of Accommodations: Case Studies
presenter: Cory Voss; Katelyn Gash; Jeneba Hoene
presenter_affiliation: Center for Disability, Accessibility & Resources, Dean of Students
description: This interactive session uses real case studies to explore the purpose and practice of disability-related accommodations and how they support student success. Participants examine instructional barriers, distinguish essential requirements from flexible practices, and learn how Universal Design for Learning strategies can reduce the need for reactive accommodations.
subject: accommodations;UDL;accessibility

title: Abstract: The 'Why' of Accommodations with Case Studies
presenter_affiliation: Center for Disability Access and Resources, University of Idaho
description: Extended abstract presenting a barrier-removal framework for understanding disability accommodations under the ADA and Section 504. Uses structured case studies that examine functional limitations, course-design barriers, approved accommodations, and rationale, helping faculty distinguish essential learning outcomes from instructional methods. Introduces Universal Design for Learning as a way to proactively reduce barriers and minimize the need for individual accommodations.
subject: disability accommodations;ADA;Section 504;Universal Design for Learning;UDL;academic integrity;accessibility;faculty development;case studies

title: Slides: The 'Why' of Accommodations with Case Studies
presenter: Cory Voss; Katelyn Gash; Jeneba Hoene
presenter_affiliation: Center for Disability Access and Resources, University of Idaho; Dean of Students, University of Idaho
description: Workshop slides presenting a barrier-removal framework for disability accommodations grounded in the ADA and Section 504. Walks through case studies analyzing functional limitations, course-design barriers, and the rationale for approved accommodations, and introduces Universal Design for Learning strategies that proactively reduce the need for reactive accommodations.
subject: disability accommodations;ADA;Section 504;Universal Design for Learning;UDL;academic integrity;accessibility;faculty development;case studies

title: How to Create a Positive Undergraduate Research Environment
presenter_affiliation: Office of Undergraduate Research; Chemistry, College of Science
description: This 60-minute workshop presents best practices for mentoring and engaging undergraduates in research at a research-intensive university. The session covers designing feasible projects, setting expectations, managing time and safety, and clarifying roles within research teams.
subject: undergraduate research;mentorship;best practices

title: Nurturing Academic Hope: Increasing Student Success Through an Active Framework of Hope
presenter_affiliation: Advising Services, SEM
description: Hope is often thought of as a passive wish. Psychological and student development research give us an alternative framing of hope as an active concept, incorporating intention, reflection, and practice. This workshop introduces the framework of academic hope, explores its components, and inspires ideas for supporting students.
subject: academic hope;well-being;persistence
